sourceMovie.title: Drifting Home
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Drifting Home (2022) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Hiroyasu Ishida's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Castle in the Sky
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Drifting Home clicked for you, Castle in the Sky (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Drifting Home?
faq.answer: Try Castle in the Sky, Children Who Chase Lost Voices, That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime the Movie: Scarlet Bond—each shares DNA with Drifting Home while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Drifting Home?
faq.answer: Drifting Home (2022) is best described as Animation, Fantasy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Drifting Home worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
